script_goals.description: Learn pinyin and hanzi simultaneously from the start, gradually shifting toward reading hanzi (or pinyin) independently without the other as a crutch. Answers accepted in either pinyin or hanzi — typed input makes requiring both impractical.

phases.label: Foundations
phases.focus: All vocabulary presented with both pinyin and hanzi side by side

phases.label: Recognition
phases.focus: Recognize common hanzi (100-300 chars); pinyin still available as support

phases.label: Transition
phases.focus: Hanzi-first exercises; pinyin shown only on request or after errors

phases.label: Independence
phases.focus: Read hanzi without pinyin scaffold; pinyin only for new vocabulary

achievements.name: First hanzi answers produced in session
achievements.description: First hanzi answers produced in session

always_include: literal/directional translation for multi-character words (e.g. 高兴 = high + spirit = happy)
character breakdown: subcomponents, radical, and mnemonic imagery or historical origin for each character
vocabulary_introduction.format_example: 再见 (zàijiàn) — 再 again + 见 see/meet → 'see you again'. 再: a second stroke added above 一, suggesting repetition. 见: eye (目) with legs — someone walking into view.

scoring_feedback.always_append_after_score: pinyin pronunciation of the word or phrase just answered, so learner keeps pronunciation top of mind even in text-only sessions
